steps.title: Write in slope-intercept form
steps.content: Since $(0,9)$ is on the line, the $y$-intercept is $9$. The equation is $$y = 8x + 9.$$ Check: at $x=1$, $y = 8(1)+9 = 17$. ✓

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: $y=\frac{1}{8}x+9$. Wrong slope — gives $y(1) = 9.125$, not $17$.
  • B: $y=x+\frac{1}{8}$. Wrong slope and intercept — gives $y(0)=0.125$, not $9$.
  • C: $y=x+8$. Slope $1$ is wrong — gives $y(1)=9$, not $17$.
  • D: $y=8x+9$. Slope $8$ and intercept $9$ match both points. Correct.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
